title: The people behind the papers - Owen Funk and David Fay.
abstract: Cell fusion is vital for multi-nucleated cells to form during development. However, whether the process of cell fusion itself contributes to developmental gene regulation programmes is unclear. In a recent study, Owen Funk, Daniel (Dan) Levy and David Fay found that disrupting cell fusion protein EFF-1 in the multi-nucleated Caenorhabditis elegans epidermis impeded the transcriptional switch from embryonic to larval gene expression programmes and caused developmental delays, suggesting that cell fusion might regulate developmental progression. To learn more about how this paper developed and the people behind it, we talked to first author Owen Funk, and one of the corresponding authors, David Fay, Professor at the Department of Molecular Biology, University of Wyoming, WY, USA.
